description: V2 oracle test facts for stoichiometric conservation laws in chemical reaction networks. Length-balanced distractors (ratio 0.8-1.2x).

facts.context: For a chemical reaction network with stoichiometry matrix S, conservation laws come from:
facts.truth: the left null space of S (vectors w where w^T S = 0)
distractors: the column space of S (vectors v in the image of S)
the right null space of S (vectors in the kernel)
the singular value decomposition of the Jacobian J

facts.context: For a reversible reaction A <-> B, the conserved quantity is:
distractors: [A] - [B] = constant (net difference conservation)
[A] / [B] = constant (equilibrium ratio preserved)
[A] * [B] = constant (product coupling invariant)

facts.context: In Michaelis-Menten enzyme kinetics E + S <-> ES -> E + P, the conserved quantities are:
distractors: [E] + [S] = constant (free enzyme plus substrate) and [ES] + [P] = constant (bound complex plus product)
[E] * [S] / [ES] = constant (binding equilibrium ratio) and [S] - [P] = constant (net substrate balance)
[E] + [P] = constant (enzyme plus product pool) and [S] + [ES] - [P] = constant (modified substrate sum)

facts.context: The number of independent conservation laws in a chemical network equals:
facts.truth: n_species minus rank(S) where S is the stoichiometry matrix
distractors: n_reactions minus rank(S) where S is the stoichiometry matrix
n_complexes minus rank(J) where J is the network Jacobian
n_species minus n_reactions plus the number of linkage classes
the instantaneous free energy flux
a transiently conserved redox moiety
the net phosphorylation potential
the net electron transfer flux
a transiently bound adenine pool
the total oxidation state balance

facts.context: For open chemical systems with inflow/outflow of species:
facts.truth: stoichiometric conservation laws may be broken by external fluxes
distractors: stoichiometric conservation laws remain exactly valid at all times
additional conservation laws emerge from the boundary flow terms
conservation laws hold provided the system reaches a steady state

facts.context: Under mass-action kinetics, the rate of reaction A + B -> C is:
facts.truth: k * [A] * [B] where k is the rate constant
distractors: k * [C] / ([A] + [B]) as the reverse equilibrium
k * ([A] + [B]) where k is the rate constant
k * [A]^2 * [B]^2 scaled by stoichiometric factors

facts.context: The Lotka-Volterra predator-prey system (viewed as chemical network) has:
facts.truth: no stoichiometric conservation laws (open system with birth/death)
distractors: one stoichiometric conservation law (total population invariant)
two stoichiometric conservation laws (predator and prey totals)
a nonlinear conservation law (product of population densities)

facts.truth: certain linear combinations of intermediate and product concentrations
distractors: the total concentration of all species summed at every time point
the ratio of intermediate to product concentrations at equilibrium
the squared amplitude of oscillations throughout the reaction cycle

facts.context: The deficiency of a chemical reaction network is:
facts.truth: n - l - s where n = complexes, l = linkage classes, s = rank(S)
distractors: n - r - s where n = species, r = reactions, s = rank of Jacobian
r - l - n where r = reactions, l = linkage classes, n = complexes
s - n + l where s = rank(S), n = species, l = connected components

facts.context: For glycolysis with 14 species and 12 reactions, the number of independent conservation laws is:
facts.truth: at least 2-5 depending on network structure (ATP/ADP and NAD/NADH pools)
distractors: exactly 14 since each species contributes one independent conservation law
exactly 12 since each reaction produces one stoichiometric constraint
exactly zero because glycolysis is an open pathway with no closed cycles

facts.context: To verify a proposed conservation law w*c = constant numerically:
facts.truth: integrate the ODE system and check that w*c(t) has zero variance
distractors: compute the eigenvalues of the Jacobian and verify all are negative
evaluate the stoichiometry matrix determinant for exact cancellation
check that all reaction rates are balanced at the initial time point

facts.truth: moieties or atomic groups that are rearranged but not created or destroyed
distractors: thermodynamic free energy balances that constrain the reaction direction
equilibrium constants arising from the principle of detailed balance
kinetic rate constraints that limit the maximum velocity of reactions

facts.truth: the set of all concentrations reachable from an initial condition via the network dynamics
distractors: the set of all equilibrium concentrations accessible under different rate constant values
the collection of initial conditions that produce identical steady state concentrations
the set of all stoichiometry matrices that share the same left null space dimension

facts.context: A non-trivial conservation law in a large network (20+ species) is typically:
facts.truth: a linear combination involving many species with non-obvious coefficients
distractors: a pairwise sum of exactly two species with unit stoichiometric weights
a product of concentration ratios forming a dimensionless equilibrium
a quadratic polynomial of species concentrations with integer exponents
